text_or_summary: # Evidence README ## Checked Evidence Pointers - Source PDF: `raw/paper__hattie-et-al-1996-learning-skills-interventions-meta-analysis.pdf` - Raw Markdown sidecar: `rawmarkdown_full/paper__hattie-et-al-1996-learning-skills-interventions-meta-analysis.md` - Checked layer: first-page title, abstract, keywords/overview, and current ARA logic layers. ## Abstract-Level Facts Captured - Learning-skills interventions are strongest when they are situated in meaningful content and tasks, rather than taught as isolated generic techniques. - Metacognitive awareness and active learner engagement are important conditions for transfer beyond simple mnemonic performance. - The SOLO model helps describe in…

# Level 3 Study Profile | Field | Value | |---|---| | APA | Hattie, J., Biggs, J., & Purdie, N. (1996). Effects of learning skills interventions on student learning: A meta-analysis.
